prompts: A triangular pyramid, or tetrahedron, has a net consisting of four equilateral triangles: one for the base and three attached to each side of the base. A cube's net consists of six equal squares arranged such that one square is central with the other five attached to its edges. This can look like a "T", with the central square in the middle and a square on each side, or in several other formations ensuring all squares are connected edge-to-edge, ultimately forming a three-dimensional cube.
